care.summary: A bold houseplant featuring thick, glossy, large leaves. It can grow into a large tree indoors if given enough space and light.
care.light: Bright, indirect light is ideal. Can tolerate some direct morning sun.
care.water: Water when the top few inches of soil are dry. It is somewhat drought tolerant.
care.humidity: Average room humidity is fine, but it appreciates occasional misting or wiping leaves with a damp cloth.
care.fertilizer: Feed every month during the growing season with a balanced liquid fertilizer.
care.toxicity: Toxic to dogs and cats, causing oral irritation and vomiting if ingested.
common_issues: Dust accumulation on the large leaves, which hinders photosynthesis
tips: Wipe the large leaves with a damp cloth to keep them glossy and dust-free.
Prune the top to encourage a bushier shape rather than a single tall stalk.
